[Affordability Confirmation] To Claudia Torres,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. Hi Jordan, we accept most banks but not online banks such as Chime and Cashapp, as we can't verify the cosigner's identity through these companies. We also can't accept joint accounts between borrowers and cosigners, or prepaid accounts. Jordan must provide an account that is open, valid, in their name and capable of ACH payments. Ask Jordan to add a suitable bank here: https://tcrdt.com/s/R5EF1LX4 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0621330016010822070907] Hi Jordan,

I hope this email finds you well.

Upon review, I can see it looks like the account you provided does not belong to you, based on our verification checks. Please review your details here: https://tcrdt.com/s/LFD1L4O1 and update anything that is incorrect. 

Once finished, to continue with your loan application, you will need to provide us with a longstanding checking account (open at least 6 months), that is active, valid, in your name, and capable of ACH payments. 

Please note that you cannot use a prepaid account or joint account with the borrower, and online-only banks such as Chime and Cash App are not accepted from cosigners since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies.

Please, use this link to continue https://tcrdt.com/s/R5EF1LX4

If you need any further help, please let us know.

Thanks,
Together Loans

Hi Jordan. Thank you for reaching out. Upon review, I can see that we are having trouble verifying the name on the account your cosigner is trying to add. This error can happen for diverse reasons. If the account is a joint account between you and your cosigner, it won't be accepted. Also, the name entered into the application portal must appear exactly how it appears on the bank account or check. This includes the spelling, punctuation, and order of how the name appears. Please ask your cosigner to check their details, we have sent them an email with more information. Thanks, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0621330016010822070907] Hi Jordan,

I hope this email finds you well.

Upon review, I can see that we are having trouble verifying the name on the account your cosigner is trying to add. This error can happen for diverse reasons. If the account is a joint account between you and your cosigner, it won't be accepted. Also, the name entered into the application portal must appear exactly how it appears on the bank account or check. This includes the spelling, punctuation, and order of how the name appears. Please ask your cosigner to check their details, we have sent them an email with more information. 

To continue with your loan application, your cosigner will need to provide us with a longstanding checking account (open at least 6 months), that is active, valid, in their name, and capable of ACH payments. 

Note that they cannot use a prepaid account or joint account with you, and make sure they are entering the ACH routing number associated with their account and not the wire-transferring routing number. Also, please note that online-only banks such as Chime and Cash App are less likely to be accepted from cosigners since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies. 

You can always check your application status here: https://tcrdt.com/s/LWUWLIOT
  
If you need any further help, please let us know.

Thanks,
Together Loans

[Affordability Confirmation] To Alisha Guthrie,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

Hi Brian, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tglns.com/s/FV4KPC9H - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Hi Jeanne, Together Loans requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/35CHMQD7 Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Jeanne, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/P2LGES7B - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Hi Anita, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! interactions.body: Hello CORMAINE. Thank you for reaching out. We received a voice message from you that you may have intended for someone else. Do you need any help with your loan application? I'm sorry, but Jonathan wasn't a suitable cosigner at this time. We have a responsibility to make sure that cosigning your loan is something that they would be able to financially support and based on the information provided we don't feel that this is the case for them. But we've already accepted your application and want to help you get the money you need - you just need to find someone else to be your cosigner instead. A good cosigner has a credit score of 700+, and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners (not tenants) are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. To continue with your loan application, please use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/W9ZAYBI1 or let us know if you need more help. Thank you, Together Loans

[RE:You asked us to help with $4500 - and we can!] Hi Melanie, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/VLOJYJNT

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[RE:Need help finding a cosigner?] Hi Jennifer, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/F0JLGJF8

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,

[Affordability Confirmation] To Luis Barillas,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Luis Barillas
IP: 146.75.164.1
DateTime (UTC): 02-15-2025
Stated Monthly Income: Self Employed $1500
Rent: $500
Bills: $250
Other Expenses: $195
Your Credit Commitments: $0
Transform Credit Repayment: $237.620000
To be safe: $50
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $267.380000


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

Hi Luis, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/5806J3PZ - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi Luis, our loans may be more expensive than bank loans, but we've been able to help thousands of people who've been unable to borrow from their bank. Our interest rate can be 25 times cheaper than a payday loan, and is often comparable to a credit card. Whilst a suitable cosigner may be able to lend you the money directly, you wouldn't benefit from us reporting your on-time and in-full repayments back to Experian, which would pave the way for better financial opportunities in the future. Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Luis,  Thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us.  We offer cosigner loans of $3,000 to $10,000 over 2 to 5 years, depending on how much you will borrow and the state you are located in. Our interest is calculated daily, so any additional payment could save you money in the long run. We recommend all of our customers pay as much as they can afford. You can make your payments before your selected due date and there is no cost for doing so.   You can change your loan amount anytime up to pay-out. Your current loan amount is $3000. To amend your details and change this loan amount, you can use this link: https://transformcredit.com/details?aRef=0625081805343206050325  If you have any other questions, just let us know.  Thanks, Transform Credit
Hi Luis, please take your time, we'll keep your application open for you as long as you need. When you're ready to continue, click here: https://tcrdt.com/s/L9QD5VFI Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0625081805343206050325] Hi Luis 

Thank you for contacting Together Loans, your message is really important to us. Our phone number is 470-435-6300 where you can reach our interactive automated assistant. 

We can help you by email, hello@togetherloans.com, or SMS. Please reply to this message with your question, and we will answer as soon as possible. 

Remember to use the same email or phone number you registered in your application - if you need to use a different contact, you can mention your loan reference number: 0625081805343206050325 with your questions.

Thanks, 
Michael@Together Loans

Hi Luis, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/AD2E8215 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Hi Luis, based on your situation, we require you to provide a cosigner. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to pay, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. If you could get a loan elsewhere without a cosigner, we might not be the right choice for you. If you have a suitable cosigner send them this link, they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/AD2E8215 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Luis, we offer loans between $3,000 and $10,000 over 2 to 5 years, depending on how much you borrow and the state you live in. It’s important to note that our interest is calculated daily, so you can settle early, make a large, one-off overpayment or regular overpayments, which will reduce your balance and save you money in the long run. You can amend your details or change your loan amount here: https://tcrdt.com/s/GK0ZPGNU We're a cosigner-based lender, meaning all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 700+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home is much more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score as homeownership is a strong indicator of financial responsibility. Thanks, Together Loans

[RE:CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Sara Maris,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 02/03/2026. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/OMT0ZQYX

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Together Loans CreditBuilder

The due date for this account remains the 17th of every month. While there was a notation regarding your borrower’s intention to make a payment on September 21, 2025, please note that this was a one-time payment arrangement and does not permanently change the scheduled due date.

Best,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

[RE:You asked us to help with $3000 - and we can!] Hi Rudolph, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/WWEFFSQL - we'd love for your loan to add to the $25million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0610140135092823051700] Hi Magali,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on June 28th, when you applied for this loan of $6000, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”. I've attached a screenshot of this page to this email. 

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Transform CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you at June 28th to magalifdz@gmail.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureaus as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. 

We also sent you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment, to your email, reminding you that your $5 payment was due. This again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product.

Based on the above, we believe we acted in line with your instructions. However, we now understand that you may not have intentionally signed-up to CreditBuilder, and, so as a gesture of goodwill, we have issued you a refund to put you back in the position you were in, before opening your CreditBuilder account. 

We will also close your account, meaning we will not attempt any further payments.

We understand that confusion can sometimes arise and we appreciate your patience as we work to rectify this situation. Please allow 3 business days for the refund to show in your account.  

Many thanks,
Denzel@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0601000303092200232621] Hi Spencer Spencer,

Unfortunately, online banks are less likely to be accepted since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies.

When the loan is ready to be paid out, the $3250 will be deposited into your cosigner's bank account for them to give to you, so it's important we have these details. 

We do this to be absolutely sure that the cosigner is aware of the loan. Additionally, because the cosigner agrees to make any payments that you don't, we can use these details as a backup method of payment if the account goes past due. 

Unfortunately, they will not be able to move forward with their cosigner application without this information. Would you like to cancel their application as a cosigner so you can add a different cosigner?

Thanks, Rebekah@TransformCredit

[RE: Missing Subject] Hi Flint,

Based on your situation, we require you to provide a cosigner. 

A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay the loan, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. We offer loans between $3000 and $10,000, depending on where you live and how long you wish to make the repayments for.

If you could get a loan elsewhere at a cheaper rate, without the need for a cosigner, it suggests that Together Loans might not be the right choice for you. You should also go with the best option on the market for you. 

If you do have a suitable cosigner, send them this link and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/DUUYDQZG

[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0609011708142505072430] Good afternoon. I know we have started the process for funding this loan.
My co-signer Kodi has decided to just give me the $ amount as a personal
loan from a friend. Can we now cancel the process?I understand if not. I
apologize for the inconvenience but this will work out better for me.

James, your loan has already been sent up, reviewed and approved. You funds will be paid out today. James, this loan will help you build your credit, a loan from a friend will not raise your score. Give us a call so we can talk about how this loan will help you better in the long run. 470.435.6300
